howItWorks.intro: This blood pressure calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Categories from American Heart Association 2024 guidelines
howItWorks.outro: Health metrics are general guidelines. Consult your healthcare provider for personalized medical advice.

faq.question: What is normal blood pressure?
faq.answer: Normal is below 120/80 mmHg. The top number (systolic) measures pressure when the heart beats. The bottom (diastolic) measures pressure between beats.

faq.question: What causes high blood pressure?
faq.answer: Risk factors include age, family history, obesity, high sodium diet, lack of exercise, stress, smoking, and excessive alcohol. Often called the silent killer because it has no symptoms.

faq.question: How often should I check?
faq.answer: At least once a year for adults with normal BP. More frequently if elevated. Home monitoring with a validated cuff is recommended for those with high BP.

faq.question: Can lifestyle changes lower BP?
faq.answer: Yes — reducing sodium to under 2,300mg/day, exercising 150 min/week, maintaining healthy weight, limiting alcohol, and managing stress can lower BP by 5-15 mmHg.

faq.question: Is the Blood Pressure Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Blood Pressure Calculator?
sections.body: The Blood Pressure Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your systolic, diastolic, you get instant results including category, risk level, recommended action. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting category right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your systolic and need to find the right category. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Systolic (top number)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your systolic in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Diastolic (bottom number)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your diastolic in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your category, risk level, recommended action. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.
